processSteps.title: Coastal Sandy Soil
processSteps.desc: The saline sandy dunes of Thanh Phu — giving a subtle saltiness no other region can match. Holds a Geographical Indication from Vietnam's IP Office.

processSteps.title: Bagged from Bud
processSteps.desc: Each fruit is individually bagged in fabric from the size of a fingertip — ensuring flawless skin, no pests, and even ripening.

faqs.a: Xoài Tứ Quý (Tu Quy Mango) is a specialty mango variety from Ben Tre that bears fruit year-round (3 seasons/year). Grown on saline sandy coastal soil in Thanh Phu, it develops a unique subtle saltiness on the finish that no other region can replicate. It holds Geographical Indication GI #00124 from Vietnam's Intellectual Property Office, issued in 2022.

faqs.q: How much does Ben Tre Tu Quy Mango cost?
faqs.a: Prices change daily: VIP 23,000–25,000₫/kg, Grade 1 20,000–22,000₫/kg, Grade 2 16,000–18,000₫/kg. Call Mr. Phuc at 0932 585 533 for today's exact price — updated every morning.

faqs.q: Will mangoes bruise during long-distance shipping?
faqs.a: No. Each fruit is individually wrapped in foam mesh and packed in padded cardboard boxes. Three years shipping to Northern Vietnam, damage rate under 2%. If a fruit is bruised — photo to Zalo, we credit your next order immediately, no questions asked.

faqs.q: How do I know the mango is really from Ben Tre?
faqs.a: Each box has a QR traceability code: farm name, harvest date, picker identity. The product falls under the Geographical Indication zone "Ben Tre" granted by the IP Office in 2022. Using this designation for mangoes from other regions is illegal.

faqs.q: Is Tu Quy Mango better ripe or unripe?
faqs.a: Both are delicious — it depends on preference. Northern Vietnamese prefer ripe — rich sweetness, strong aroma, subtle saltiness. Southern Vietnamese prefer unripe — crisp, pleasantly tart. We carry both.

faqs.q: What is the minimum for wholesale Ben Tre mango?
faqs.a: Minimum 1 box of 20kg. Packed to your preference — cardboard, foam, or mesh bag. Shipping quoted upfront by route. Call 0932 585 533 for pricing.
COD on delivery, or bank transfer upfront. Regular buyers who've placed 3 orders can open a credit account. Full VAT invoices available for restaurants and businesses.

faqs.q: When is Tu Quy Mango season?
faqs.a: Tu Quy Mango bears fruit year-round with 3 main seasons: lunar month 4, lunar month 8, and lunar month 12 (Tet season). Green mango is available year-round. We have stock every month — never out.

hero.desc: The premium variant of Tu Quy — stunning golden skin, melt-in-the-mouth sweetness like Cat Hoa Loc, almost no fiber. Same salt-touched Thanh Phu soil, but far rarer — when it's gone, wait for next season.

sellingPoints.title: Beautiful golden skin

sellingPoints.title: Melt-sweet, fiber-free
sellingPoints.desc: Ripe sweetness like Cat Hoa Loc. Almost zero fiber — silky flesh melts on the tongue.

sellingPoints.title: Thanh Phu subtle saltiness
sellingPoints.desc: Retains the characteristic subtle saltiness from salt-infused sandy soil — authentic Tu Quy DNA.

sellingPoints.title: Rare variety, cold delivery
sellingPoints.desc: Limited harvest — when it's gone, wait for next season. Cold-chain nationwide — HCM 24h, HN 48h.

compare.title: Hoang Kim vs Regular Tu Quy

items.a: The premium variant of Tu Quy Mango — deep golden skin when ripe, melt-sweet flesh with almost no fiber. Same Tu Quy variety, same Thanh Phu origin, but far rarer.

items.q: How is Hoang Kim different from regular Tu Quy?
items.a: Deep golden skin (vs green-yellow). Smoother flesh, sweeter, near zero fiber. Larger fruit (800g-1.5kg). Price 35-45k/kg (1.5-2x more expensive).

items.q: Why is Hoang Kim more expensive?
items.a: Very limited harvest — only select orchards have this variety. Beautiful large fruit, superior flesh quality. Rare = premium pricing.
No — follows Tu Quy seasons (Apr-Jun, Aug-Oct, Dec-Feb) but with far less volume. When it's gone, wait for next season. Order early to reserve.

items.q: Can I use Hoang Kim as a gift?
items.a: Perfect for gifting — gorgeous golden fruit, substantial weight (800g+), melt-sweet. A 5kg box holds 4-6 fruits, more impressive than regular VIP.

items.q: Can I order Ben Tre young coconut online with nationwide shipping?
items.a: Yes. We ship cold-chain nationwide: HCM 24h, Hanoi 48h, Da Nang 36h. Order via Zalo or call 0932 585 533.

items.q: How is a coconut shell different from a whole coconut?
items.a: A coconut shell is a young coconut fully peeled of its green husk — just the white shell with water inside. Open the cap and drink immediately. Beautifully packaged, easy to ship long distance.

items.q: How much does Ben Tre young coconut cost?
items.a: Wholesale 50+: 8,000-10,000₫/coconut. Retail 10+: 15,000-18,000₫/coconut. Prices may vary seasonally — call for accurate quote.
Ben Tre young coconut water is naturally sweet, not astringent. We select coconuts at peak ripeness — sweetest possible.

items.q: How long can coconut shells be stored?
items.a: Unopened shell: 5-7 days refrigerated. Once opened: drink same day. Vacuum-packed: 10-14 days refrigerated.

internalLinks.label: Ben Tre vs Mekong Delta Coconut

hero.desc: Only Thanh Phu in Vietnam consistently produces top-quality Tu Quy Mango — thanks to naturally salt-infused coastal sandy soil.
hero.mapTitle: Thanh Phong, Thanh Phu, Ben Tre location

vuongTrong.label: Part A
vuongTrong.title: Thanh Phu Growing Region
vuongTrong.locationDesc: Thanh Phu district — one of three coastal districts in Ben Tre province, at the Mekong Delta estuary. The growing area sits on coastal sand ridges 2–4m above sea level.
vuongTrong.historyDesc1: The Tu Quy Mango variety originated in 1982, bred by farmers in Phu Da hamlet, Vinh Binh commune, Cho Lach district. Through farming experience, only the salt-infused coastal sandy soil of Thanh Phu produces the highest-quality fruit.
vuongTrong.historyDesc2: Thanh Phu previously grew cassava and watermelon — unstable income. From 2010, many families switched to mango farming, increasing income 3–4 times.

factors.title: Low water content
factors.subtitle: Crisp & firm flesh
factors.desc: Sandy soil retains little water → low H₂O in the fruit → crisp when green, firm when ripe. A natural advantage requiring no root-restriction techniques.

factors.title: Salt-infused soil
factors.subtitle: Signature subtle saltiness
factors.desc: 0.009–0.022% dissolved salt in soil → sodium (Na) accumulates in fruit → subtle saltiness on the finish. This characteristic cannot be replicated elsewhere.

factors.title: Sweet-salt interplay
factors.subtitle: Perfect flavor balance
factors.desc: Fresh water from the Mekong + salt water from the East Sea → rich sweetness blended with subtle saltiness — found nowhere else.

quyTrinh.label: Part C
quyTrinh.title: Production Process

steps.title: Soil preparation
steps.desc: Select elevated sandy terrain, apply organic base fertilizer, install drip irrigation system.

steps.title: Planting & care
steps.desc: Grafted seedlings, fruiting in 15–18 months. Regular watering, organic fertilizer, canopy pruning.

steps.title: Early fruit bagging
steps.desc: Bag each fruit in colored cloth bags when thumbnail-sized. Each batch gets one color to distinguish maturity.

steps.title: Harvest & sorting
steps.desc: Harvest by bag color, cut stems, sort at the orchard by size: VIP/Grade 1/Grade 2.

steps.title: Processing & packing
steps.desc: Wipe clean, second-sort, pack in foam-lined carton boxes, attach traceability sticker.

steps.title: Shipping
steps.desc: Cold truck, standard truck or air freight — per buyer preference. Ben Tre → Hanoi: ~2 days.

quyTrinh.bagTechTitle: Fruit Bagging Technique — Thanh Phu's Signature
quyTrinh.bagTechDesc: Trees fruit year-round — a single tree holds fruits at all stages simultaneously. Farmers bag each fruit in cloth from thumbnail size, using different colors per batch. The result: beautiful uniform fruit, 5–10 kg less pesticide per hectare, 18–20 million VND profit increase per hectare.

items.title: Geographical Indication #00124
items.org: Vietnam IP Office — Ministry of Science & Technology
items.detail: Decision No. 5371/QD-SHTT dated 10/11/2022. Only Tu Quy Mango grown in Thanh Phu, Ba Tri, Binh Dai may be called 'Tu Quy Mango Ben Tre'.

items.title: Certification trademark
items.detail: The 'Xoai Tu Quy Thanh Phu' trademark has been protected since 2020. Distributed through markets, supermarkets, and e-commerce platforms.

items.title: VietGAP — 100+ ha
items.org: Ben Tre Department of Crop Production & Plant Protection
items.detail: Over 100 ha in Thanh Phong commune meet VietGAP standards. Regular farmer training conducted.

items.title: GlobalGAP — 16 ha
items.detail: Thanh Phong Co-op has 16 ha certified GlobalGAP. Products sell at 1,500–2,000₫/kg premium above market.

items.title: Export growing area code
items.org: Plant Protection Department — Ministry of Agriculture
items.detail: 86 ha in Thanh Phong granted PUC codes — officially exported to the US, South Korea, and Australia.

items.title: OCOP Ben Tre
items.detail: Xoai Tu Quy Thanh Phu is a flagship OCOP product of Ben Tre province.

veVua.desc: From Tu Quy Mango to young coconut — everything from Thanh Phu farm
veVua.body1: Ben Tre Fruits Farm started with Tu Quy Mango — the GI #00124 specialty variety found only in Thanh Phu, Ba Tri, and Binh Dai. After 3 years of nationwide delivery, we expanded to Ben Tre Young Coconut (pre-peeled shell) and Coconut Jelly Coconut — specialties from Vietnam's most famous coconut region.
veVua.body2: Our promise: direct from growers, no middlemen, 2% extra weight to cover natural loss, damaged goods refunded on next order. Everyone says this — but we've been doing it for real, 3 years running.
